title: seriphium cernuum
intro: Seriphium cernuum, also known as the mountain tea or Kaffedal, is a shrubby perennial plant native to South Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Seriphium cernuum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water or as a tincture using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
